steps.title: Turn the conditions into two rows
steps.content: For a linear function, $g(x+1)-g(x)=6$ means every time $x$ increases by $1$, the function value increases by $6$. Since $g(4)=10$, the next point is $g(5)=16$.

steps.title: Find the line from the data
steps.content: Using $(4,10)$ and $(5,16)$, the slope is $$m=\frac{16-10}{5-4}=6.$$ Substitute $(4,10)$ into $y=6x+b$: $$10=24+b,$$ so $b=-14$. The equation is $y=6x-14$.

steps.title: Confirm the choice
steps.content: Choice A and choice B have slope $4$, so they do not satisfy $g(x+1)-g(x)=6$. Choice D has slope $6$, but $g(4)=6(4)-24=0$, not $10$. Choice C, $g(x)=6x-14$, has $g(4)=10$ and increases by $6$ each step. Choice C is correct.
